roomEntry: The floorboards creak as you step into the {room}.
You squeeze through a gap into the {room}.
Dust hangs in the air of the {room}.
You catch your breath and take in the {room}.
Not much to look at, but it's a {room}.
lootSuccess: You rifle through the debris and pull out {item}.
Your hand closes around {item} in the dark.
lootFailure: Whatever was here, someone got to it first.
You turn the place over. Not a thing.
enemyEncounter: Something shifts in the shadows ahead.
You freeze — something's in here with you.
A sound behind you; you spin around.
You almost walk straight into it.
The air goes cold. You're not alone.
You hear breathing. It isn't yours.
escapeSuccess: You bolt and don't look back.
You find a gap and take it.
Legs carry you out before your brain catches up.
Close. Too close. But you're out.
You scramble free and keep moving.
escapeFailure: No good — it's right behind you.
It cuts you off. You take the hit.
The exit's blocked. You eat the damage.
playerHit: That one hurt it. {damage} damage dealt.
playerTakesHit: It hits you for {damage}. That stings.
{damage} damage. You stagger but stay up.
You don't dodge fast enough. {damage} taken.
It catches you clean. {damage} HP gone.
trapRoom: The floor shifts — a trap. {damage} damage.
Too late to dodge it. {damage} damage.
You walk right into it. {damage} damage.
The ceiling drops something heavy. {damage} damage.
darkRoom: Pitch black. You can't see a thing.
No light in here at all.
Complete darkness. You'd need a torch to search this.
You can barely see your hand in front of your face.
Dark as anything. Not looting without a light.
The dark in here is total.
floodedRoom: Water on the floor — ankle deep.
The floor's soaked. Something got in here.
A leak somewhere above. The floor's wet through.
You step in before you see the water.
merchant: A figure with a bag eyes you from the corner.
Someone's set up shop in here. Bold choice.
You weren't expecting company. Neither were they.
A trader. Of sorts. They nod at you.
You clock the merchandise before you clock the person.
They've got gear. You've got money. Maybe.
scavenger: A scavenger picks through the debris nearby.
Someone's here before you, sifting through the wreck.
They look up. They want what you've got, not the other way round.
A buyer, not a seller. Interesting.
They're not selling. But they're definitely interested in your kit.
You've got things they want. Whether you sell is up to you.
armourBreak: Your {tier} {slot} finally gives out, crumbling into {materials}.
The {tier} {slot} can't take any more — it falls apart.
One hit too many. Your {tier} {slot} is done.
You hear a crack. The {tier} {slot} is finished.
That's it for the {tier} {slot}. It breaks into {materials}.
